title: Meibomia trifoliastra
taxonomy: Meibomia trifoliastra is a member of the legume family (Fabaceae) and is classified as a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the genus Meibomia, which is a group of plants native to North America. Meibomia trifoliastra is further classified as a type of legume, which is a broad category of plants that have fruit in the form of pods. The species is closely related to other plants in the Fabaceae family, which includes beans, peas, and clover. Meibomia trifoliastra is a relatively rare species, but its classification provides valuable information about its evolutionary history and relationships to other plants.

intro: Meibomia trifoliastra, also known as Trifolium pratense or wild red clover, is a perennial legume native to North America, characterized by its trifoliate leaves and pink to purple flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of menopause, as well as its antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. The bioactive constituents of Meibomia trifoliastra include isoflavones, particularly genistein and daidzein, as well as coumarins and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Meibomia trifoliastra is commonly used in herbal preparations such as capsules, teas, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and salves for skin and wound care.
